\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[letterpaper,margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{amsmath,amssymb,amsthm,booktabs,hyperref}
\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
\newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
\newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}

\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb R}
\newcommand{\T}{\mathcal T}
\newcommand{\E}{\mathbb E}
\newcommand{\dd}{\,\mathrm d}
\title{Strict global $\mathrm{PF}_4$ for the Riemann kernel\\
\large A computer-assisted proof}
\author{falseywinchnet\\\url{https://github.com/falseywinchnet/zeta}}
\date{15 July 2026}

\begin{document}
\maketitle
\begin{abstract}
Let
\[
f(t)=\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}
\left(4\pi^2n^4e^{9|t|/2}-6\pi n^2e^{5|t|/2}\right)
e^{-\pi n^2e^{2|t|}}
\]
be the classical Riemann, or de Bruijn--Newman, kernel. We prove that $f$ is
strictly $\mathrm{PF}_4$. The computer-assisted input consists of certified
one-variable inequalities, most importantly global positivity of the fully
confluent invariant $\mathcal C_4$. A quotient-Wronskian reduction converts
arbitrary fourth-order minors into $\partial_\xi\Psi<0$, whose numerator is a
positive crossing-kernel integral.
\end{abstract}
\tableofcontents

\section{Kernel and main result}
The theta functional equation gives the smooth even continuation through
$t=0$.
\begin{theorem}[Strict global $\mathrm{PF}_4$]
For $1\le r\le4$ and strictly increasing tuples $x_1<\cdots<x_r$ and
$y_1<\cdots<y_r$,
\[
\det[f(x_i-y_j)]_{i,j=1}^r>0.
\]
\end{theorem}

\section{Certified one-variable inputs}
Put $\ell=\log f$, $s=\ell'$, and $q=-s'$. Define
\[
F_1=qq''-(q')^2,\qquad F_2=q^3-F_1,
\]
\[
A(a,b)=s(a)-s(b),\qquad
M(a,b)=\frac{q(b)-q(a)}{A(a,b)},\qquad
\Lambda(\xi;m,r)=A(\xi,r)+M(\xi,m)-M(m,r).
\]
For cumulants $c_j=\ell^{(j)}$, let
\[
\begin{gathered}
m_0=1,\ m_1=0,\ m_2=c_2,\ m_3=c_3,\
m_4=c_4+3c_2^2,\\
m_5=c_5+10c_2c_3,\quad
m_6=c_6+15c_2c_4+10c_3^2+15c_2^3,
\end{gathered}
\]
and $\mathcal C_4=\det[m_{i+j-2}]_{i,j=1}^4$.
\begin{lemma}[Certified input]
On $\R$, $q>0$, $F_2>0$, $\mathcal C_4>0$, and
$\Lambda(\xi;m,r)>0$ for $\xi<m<r$.
\end{lemma}
The compact interval was certified with directed Arb arithmetic. For
$x=\pi e^{2t}$ and $t\ge1$,
\[
c_j=-2^jx+\varepsilon_j,qquad |\varepsilon_j|\le E_j/x,
\]
with
\[
(E_2,E_3,E_4,E_5,E_6)=(19.8,176,2082,30770,545900).
\]
The order-three bound quoted in the preliminary draft is
\[
\Lambda(\xi;m,r)\ge\int_\xi^r
\frac{\min(q(t)^3,F_2(t))}{q(t)^2}\dd t>0.
\]

\section{From arbitrary minors to a three-point derivative}
For $u_j(t)=f(t-y_j)$, set
\[
v_j=(u_j/u_1)',\qquad w_j=(v_j/v_2)'.
\]
The preliminary draft records
\[
W(u_1,u_2,u_3)=u_1^3W(v_2,v_3),
\]
\[
W(u_1,u_2,u_3,u_4)=u_1^4v_2^3w_3^2
\frac{\dd}{\dd t}(w_4/w_3).
\]
For $p_j=t-y_j$, so $p_4<p_3<p_2<p_1$, define
\[
\Psi(\xi;m,r)=\Lambda(\xi;m,r)+\T\log\Lambda(\xi;m,r).
\]
\begin{proposition}[Three-point reduction]
Strict global $\mathrm{PF}_4$ follows from
$\partial_\xi\Psi(\xi;m,r)<0$ for every $\xi<m<r$.
\end{proposition}
The two displayed reductions in the reviewed draft are
\[
\T\log\frac{v_3}{v_2}
=\frac{A(p_3,p_2)}{A(p_3,p_1)}\Lambda(p_3;p_2,p_1)>0,
\]
and
\[
\T\log\frac{w_4}{w_3}
=\Psi(p_4;p_2,p_1)-\Psi(p_3;p_2,p_1).
\]

\section{Curvature coordinate}
Put $y(t)=-s(t)$, $Q(y(t))=q(t)$, and
\[
\rho=1-Q'',\qquad \kappa=2-Q''=1+\rho.
\]
For $p=y(\xi)<z=y(m)<w=y(r)$, $L=z-p$, and $R=w-z$,
\[
\Lambda=(w-p)+Q[p,z]-Q[z,w],
\]
\[
\Lambda=\frac1L\int_p^z(y-p)\kappa(y)\dd y
+\frac1R\int_z^w(w-y)\kappa(y)\dd y,
\]
\[
\delta=-\partial_p\Lambda
=\frac1{L^2}\int_p^z(z-y)\kappa(y)\dd y>0.
\]
With
\[
\mathcal N=\delta\Lambda^2+\Lambda Q'(p)\delta+
\T\delta-\delta\T\Lambda,
\]
the preliminary sign bridge is
\[
\partial_\xi\Psi=-\frac{Q(p)}{\Lambda^2}\mathcal N.
\]

\section{The transport identity}
Define probability measures
\[
\dd\mu(y)=\frac{(z-y)\kappa(y)}{L^2\delta}\mathbf1_{[p,z]}(y)\dd y,
\]
\[
\dd\nu(y)=\frac{(y-p)\kappa(y)}{L\Lambda}\mathbf1_{[p,z]}(y)\dd y
+\frac{(w-y)\kappa(y)}{R\Lambda}\mathbf1_{[z,w]}(y)\dd y.
\]
Set
\[
A_0(y)=3(y-Q'(y))-Q(y)(\log\kappa(y))',
\quad D=A_0'.
\]
\begin{lemma}[Confluent-to-curvature identity]
$\mathcal C_4=Q^6\kappa^2D$.
\end{lemma}
The preliminary draft defines
\[
H=3y^2-3Q-3yQ'+(Q')^2+QQ'',\qquad
J=y^3-3yQ+QQ',
\]
and states $\kappa A_0=H'$ and $H=J'$. Its central cancellation is
\begin{lemma}[Exact transport cancellation]
\[
\frac{\mathcal N}{\delta\Lambda}
=\E_\nu[A_0]-\E_\mu[A_0].
\]
\end{lemma}
With $I_L=\int_p^zH$ and $I_R=\int_z^wH$, the endpoint form is
\[
\E_\nu[A_0]-\E_\mu[A_0]
=\frac{-I_L/L+I_R/R}{\Lambda}+
\frac{LH(p)-I_L}{L^2\delta}.
\]

\section{Positive crossing kernel and completion}
For $W=F_\mu-F_\nu$, the preliminary draft states
\[
W(t)>0\quad(p<t<w),\qquad W(p)=W(w)=0.
\]
On $(p,z)$ its density ratio is
\[
\frac{\dd\mu/\dd y}{\dd\nu/\dd y}
=\frac{\Lambda(z-y)}{L\delta(y-p)}.
\]
Integration by parts then gives
\[
\mathcal N=\delta\Lambda\int_p^w
W(t)\,\frac{\mathcal C_4(t)}{Q(t)^6\kappa(t)^2}\dd t>0.
\]
The sign bridge and three-point reduction complete the preliminary argument.

\section{Reproducibility and focused audit}
The preliminary draft points to the PF3, confluent, Wronskian, and transport
certificate notes and their four replay scripts. It describes the compact
input as directed 192-bit Arb arithmetic and the transport replay as exact
symbolic algebra with independent high-precision kernel checks.

\appendix
\section{Exact fourth-order expansion}
The preliminary draft displays
\begin{align*}
\mathcal C_4={}&12c_2^6+24c_2^4c_4-24c_2^3c_3^2+2c_2^3c_6
-12c_2^2c_3c_5+7c_2^2c_4^2\\
&+12c_2c_3^2c_4+c_2c_4c_6-c_2c_5^2-9c_3^4\\
&-c_3^2c_6+2c_3c_4c_5-c_4^3.
\end{align*}
The full tail polynomial remains in the reviewed PDF. It also appears in
Appendix~B of the maintained paper.
